moves.description: Investigate qdrant-client==1.7.0 for correct exception import/access.
moves.details: Determine the correct way to import or access qdrant_client.http.exceptions.ResponseHandlingException or its equivalent for qdrant-client version 1.7.0. This may involve checking the library's structure or documentation for this version.
Modify the ensure_qdrant_collection_async function in src/utils.py to use the correctly identified exception type from qdrant-client==1.7.0 in the try...except block for ResponseHandlingException.

moves.description: Test application startup for AttributeError resolution.
moves.details: Rebuild and restart the application. Verify that the AttributeError is resolved and the Pydantic ResponseHandlingException (or equivalent) is caught as expected during the Qdrant collection check. Check logs for confirmation.

Confirm that if the Qdrant collection doesn't exist, it's created successfully. Confirm that if the collection already exists (even with the client-side Pydantic parsing issues), the application proceeds without attempting to recreate it and causing a conflict. This may involve manual inspection of Qdrant or specific log messages. Identify and fix the 'coroutine object is not subscriptable' error.

moves.description: Final test: Crawl a new URL and verify data in Qdrant.
moves.details: Perform a crawl of a new, non-trivial URL. Check application logs for success. If possible, inspect Qdrant to confirm new data is present and correctly structured. Close Bivvy if successful.
